2604.11705.title: Agentic Driving Coach: Robustness and Determinism of Agentic AI-Powered Human-in-the-Loop Cyber-Physical Systems
2604.11705.paper_summary: Foundation models, including large language models (LLMs), are increasingly used for human-in-the-loop (HITL) cyber-physical systems (CPS) because foundation model-based AI agents can potentially interact with both the physical environments and human users. However, the unpredictable behavior of human users and AI agents, in addition to the dynamically changing physical environments, leads to uncontrollable nondeterminism. To address this urgent challenge of enabling agentic AI-powered HITL CPS, we propose a reactor-model-of-computation (MoC)-based approach, realized by the open-source Lingua Franca (LF) framework. We also carry out a concrete case study using the agentic driving coach as an application of HITL CPS. By evaluating the LF-based agentic HITL CPS, we identify practical challenges in reintroducing determinism into such agentic HITL CPS and present pathways to address them.
2604.11705.paper_summary_zh: 基礎模型，包括大型語言模型（LLMs），越來越多地用於人機互動（HITL）網絡物理系統（CPS），因為基於基礎模型的人工智慧代理可以與物理環境和人類用戶進行互動。<lb/>然而，人類用戶和人工智慧代理的不可預測行為，加上動態變化的物理環境，導致了無法控制的非確定性。<lb/>為了解決這一緊迫挑戰，使得代理型人工智慧驅動的HITL CPS成為可能，我們提出了一種基於反應器計算模型（MoC）的方法，通過開源的Lingua Franca（LF）框架實現。<lb/>我們還進行了一個具體的案例研究，使用代理型駕駛教練作為HITL CPS的應用。<lb/>通過評估基於LF的代理型HITL CPS，我們識別了在這種代理型HITL CPS中重新引入確定性所面臨的實際挑戰，並提出了解決這些挑戰的途徑。
2604.11705.author: Deeksha Prahlad et.al.
2604.11705.authors: Deeksha Prahlad, Daniel Fan, Hokeun Kim
